notes: Auto-executed tool call, gate-less happy path for Gemini CLI. The recipe drives ONE auto-approved shell tool call ('echo hello') under `gemini -y` (yolo), then a terminal 'done' reply. Canonical arc: ready→working→ready, state NEVER enters waiting. DAEMON STORY (grounded in testdata/real-toolcall-session.jsonl + parser.go): the opening user prompt → user_message drives ready→working; the assistant 'gemini' message carrying toolCalls → ToolUses keeps the session working (content tool-calling, not terminal); the user-role functionResponse message → function_call_output closes the open tool WITHOUT settling; the final 'gemini' message with non-empty content and zero toolCalls → turn_done settles working→ready. Under yolo every tool auto-approves so PermissionPending is never set and waiting is unreachable. SESSION IDENTITY (two-tier, matched to the live recording): a transient proc-<PID> presession is born ready first (presession_birth), then the rollout session reconciles a few hundred ms later with a DIFFERENT session_id keyed on the transcript header sessionId (session_birth, new_session) — Gemini stores its transcript as a *.jsonl rollout under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/ (FilesUnderRoot), like claudecode/codex, NOT aider's proc-<PID>. So all post-birth phases (pid_bind, turn_start, turn_end) anchor same_session_as session_birth — the REAL rollout session, never the presession (which never goes working). The launcher PID binds via cwd harvested from the bootstrap <session_context> (the heap-bump V8 re-exec is excluded by isHeapBumpWorker). INTERACTIVE recipe (not headless `gemini -p`): Gemini emits no explicit end-of-turn marker, so the parser synthesizes working→ready from a terminal text turn; keeping the REPL alive across send + wait_turn plus an ≥8s trailing sleep lets the daemon observe the full settle. The phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span (turn_start→turn_end) plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase; tool visibility is coarse (call id+name + functionResponse close). NO teardown phase: the daemon emits NO process_exited for gemini-cli in coexist mode (verified absent across every gemini-cli recording) — the only teardown signal is transcript_removed on the proc-<PID> presession, not the rollout session — so asserting an exit edge would assert an event the daemon does not emit. Observations assert the websocket metric vector: cost and tokens non-zero (Gemini bills per-message input/output/cached) and agent gemini-cli; model is left unpinned because the live default is account-dependent (recording shows gemini-3.5-flash).

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready shortly after gemini -y launches
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id, keyed on the transcript header sessionId), born ready
Launcher PID bound to the rollout session via cwd from the bootstrap <session_context> (heap-bump worker excluded)
User prompt requiring a tool call → working on the rollout session (the auto-executed run_shell_command runs inside this span)
The shell tool auto-executes under yolo (no approval gate) and the terminal text turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
